Bases:
ABC
Abstract base class for streaming candidate generators.
Contract:
- Initialized with left/right DataFrames and strategy params
- Iterable yielding DataFrames with columns [“id1”, “id2”, “block_key?”].
- Each yielded batch should be <=
batch_size
rows.
-
estimate_pairs()
may return an estimate or None.
-
stats()
returns simple diagnostics.
-
materialize()
returns all candidates as a single DataFrame (for small data).

class
RuleBasedMatcher
(
BaseMatcher
):
"""Rule-based matcher using weighted linear combination of attribute similarities.
This matcher implements the same functionality as Winter's LinearCombinationMatchingRule
but with PyDI's simpler DataFrame-first design. It computes a weighted sum of
similarity scores from multiple comparators to determine entity matches.
The comparators and weights are passed directly to the match() method following
the design principle of keeping the matcher stateless.
Example
-------
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching import RuleBasedMatcher
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching.comparators import jaccard, date_within_years
>>>
>>> matcher = RuleBasedMatcher()
>>> matches = matcher.match(
...     df_left, df_right, candidates,
...     comparators=[jaccard("title"), date_within_years("date", 2)],

BaseBlocker
Token-based blocking using token overlap on a text column.
Each record is assigned to blocks by its tokens. Candidate pairs are
generated when left/right records share at least one token. A simple
tokenizer is used by default, but a custom callable may be provided.
Also supports character and word ngrams for more flexible blocking strategies.
When using ngrams, records are blocked based on overlapping character sequences
or word sequences of the specified size.

"""Rule-based matcher using weighted linear combination of attribute similarities.
This matcher implements the same functionality as Winter's LinearCombinationMatchingRule
but with PyDI's simpler DataFrame-first design. It computes a weighted sum of
similarity scores from multiple comparators to determine entity matches.
The comparators and weights are passed directly to the match() method following
the design principle of keeping the matcher stateless.
Example
-------
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching import RuleBasedMatcher
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching.comparators import jaccard, date_within_years
>>>
>>> matcher = RuleBasedMatcher()
>>> matches = matcher.match(
...     df_left, df_right, candidates,

Notes
The “relative_difference” method with max_difference is equivalent to Winter’s
PercentageSimilarity. For values differing by X%, where X < max_difference*100:
similarity = 1 - (X/100) / max_difference

BaseBlocker
Embedding-based blocking using nearest neighbor search over text embeddings.
This blocker combines text columns into a single string representation,
computes embeddings using sentence transformers or a custom embedder,
and uses nearest neighbor search to find candidate pairs above a similarity threshold.

[source]

Bases:
BaseBlocker
Token-based blocking using token overlap on a text column.
Each record is assigned to blocks by its tokens. Candidate pairs are
generated when left/right records share at least one token. A simple
tokenizer is used by default, but a custom callable may be provided.
Also supports character and word ngrams for more flexible blocking strategies.
When using ngrams, records are blocked based on overlapping character sequences
or word sequences of the specified size.

[source]

Bases:
BaseBlocker
Token-based blocking using token overlap on a text column.
Each record is assigned to blocks by its tokens. Candidate pairs are
generated when left/right records share at least one token. A simple
tokenizer is used by default, but a custom callable may be provided.
Also supports character and word ngrams for more flexible blocking strategies.
When using ngrams, records are blocked based on overlapping character sequences
or word sequences of the specified size.

Bases:
BaseBlocker
Token-based blocking using token overlap on a text column.
Each record is assigned to blocks by its tokens. Candidate pairs are
generated when left/right records share at least one token. A simple
tokenizer is used by default, but a custom callable may be provided.
Also supports character and word ngrams for more flexible blocking strategies.
When using ngrams, records are blocked based on overlapping character sequences
or word sequences of the specified size.

class
RuleBasedMatcher
(
BaseMatcher
):
"""Rule-based matcher using weighted linear combination of attribute similarities.
This matcher implements the same functionality as Winter's LinearCombinationMatchingRule
but with PyDI's simpler DataFrame-first design. It computes a weighted sum of
similarity scores from multiple comparators to determine entity matches.
The comparators and weights are passed directly to the match() method following
the design principle of keeping the matcher stateless.
Example
-------
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching import RuleBasedMatcher
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching.comparators import jaccard, date_within_years
>>>
>>> matcher = RuleBasedMatcher()
>>> matches = matcher.match(
...     df_left, df_right, candidates,
...     comparators=[jaccard("title"), date_within_years("date", 2)],


Bases:
BaseBlocker
Token-based blocking using token overlap on a text column.
Each record is assigned to blocks by its tokens. Candidate pairs are
generated when left/right records share at least one token. A simple
tokenizer is used by default, but a custom callable may be provided.
Also supports character and word ngrams for more flexible blocking strategies.
When using ngrams, records are blocked based on overlapping character sequences
or word sequences of the specified size.

"""Machine learning-based entity matcher with scikit-learn integration.
This matcher uses trained scikit-learn classifiers to predict entity matches
based on feature vectors extracted from candidate pairs. It provides minimal
wrapping around scikit-learn while maintaining PyDI compatibility.
The matcher requires a FeatureExtractor to convert entity pairs into features
and a trained scikit-learn classifier. This design allows users to leverage
the full scikit-learn ecosystem (hyperparameter tuning, cross-validation,

PyDI.entitymatching.ml_based.
MLBasedMatcher
(
feature_extractor

[source]

Bases:
BaseMatcher
Machine learning-based entity matcher with scikit-learn integration.
This matcher uses trained scikit-learn classifiers to predict entity matches
based on feature vectors extracted from candidate pairs. It provides minimal
wrapping around scikit-learn while maintaining PyDI compatibility.
The matcher requires a FeatureExtractor to convert entity pairs into features
and a trained scikit-learn classifier. This design allows users to leverage
the full scikit-learn ecosystem (hyperparameter tuning, cross-validation,
feature selection, etc.) while maintaining PyDI’s DataFrame-first approach.
Parameters
:
feature_extractor
(
FeatureExtractor
or
VectorFeatureExtractor
) – Feature extraction component that converts entity pairs to feature vectors.
Examples
>>>
from
PyDI.entitymatching
import
MLBasedMatcher
,
FeatureExtractor
>>>
from
PyDI.entitymatching.comparators
import
StringComparator
>>>
from
sklearn.ensemble
import
RandomForestClassifier
>>>
>>>
# Create feature extractor
>>>
extractor
=
FeatureExtractor

[source]

Bases:
BaseBlocker
Token-based blocking using token overlap on a text column.
Each record is assigned to blocks by its tokens. Candidate pairs are
generated when left/right records share at least one token. A simple
tokenizer is used by default, but a custom callable may be provided.
Also supports character and word ngrams for more flexible blocking strategies.
When using ngrams, records are blocked based on overlapping character sequences
or word sequences of the specified size.

class
RuleBasedMatcher
(
BaseMatcher
):
"""Rule-based matcher using weighted linear combination of attribute similarities.
This matcher implements the same functionality as Winter's LinearCombinationMatchingRule
but with PyDI's simpler DataFrame-first design. It computes a weighted sum of
similarity scores from multiple comparators to determine entity matches.
The comparators and weights are passed directly to the match() method following
the design principle of keeping the matcher stateless.

-------
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching import RuleBasedMatcher
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching.comparators import jaccard, date_within_years
>>>
>>> matcher = RuleBasedMatcher()
>>> matches = matcher.match(
...     df_left, df_right, candidates,
...     comparators=[jaccard("title"), date_within_years("date", 2)],

Embedding-based blocking using nearest neighbor search over text embeddings.
This blocker combines text columns into a single string representation,
computes embeddings using sentence transformers or a custom embedder,

class
RuleBasedMatcher
(
BaseMatcher
):
"""Rule-based matcher using weighted linear combination of attribute similarities.
This matcher implements the same functionality as Winter's LinearCombinationMatchingRule
but with PyDI's simpler DataFrame-first design. It computes a weighted sum of
similarity scores from multiple comparators to determine entity matches.
The comparators and weights are passed directly to the match() method following
the design principle of keeping the matcher stateless.
Example
-------
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching import RuleBasedMatcher
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching.comparators import jaccard, date_within_years
>>>
>>> matcher = RuleBasedMatcher()
>>> matches = matcher.match(
...     df_left, df_right, candidates,
...     comparators=[jaccard("title"), date_within_years("date", 2)],

class
PyDI.entitymatching.
RuleBasedMatcher
[source]

Bases:
BaseMatcher
Rule-based matcher using weighted linear combination of attribute similarities.
This matcher implements the same functionality as Winter’s LinearCombinationMatchingRule
but with PyDI’s simpler DataFrame-first design. It computes a weighted sum of
similarity scores from multiple comparators to determine entity matches.
The comparators and weights are passed directly to the match() method following
the design principle of keeping the matcher stateless.
Example
>>>
from
PyDI.entitymatching
import
RuleBasedMatcher
>>>
from
PyDI.entitymatching.comparators
import
jaccard
,
date_within_years
>>>
>>>
matcher
=
RuleBasedMatcher
()
>>>
matches
=
matcher
.
match
(
...
df_left
,
df_right
,
candidates
,
...
comparators
=
[
jaccard
(
"title"
),
date_within_years
(
"date"
,
2
)],

weights

2
,
1

Bases:
BaseMatcher
Machine learning-based entity matcher with scikit-learn integration.
This matcher uses trained scikit-learn classifiers to predict entity matches
based on feature vectors extracted from candidate pairs. It provides minimal
wrapping around scikit-learn while maintaining PyDI compatibility.
The matcher requires a FeatureExtractor to convert entity pairs into features
and a trained scikit-learn classifier. This design allows users to leverage
the full scikit-learn ecosystem (hyperparameter tuning, cross-validation,
feature selection, etc.) while maintaining PyDI’s DataFrame-first approach.

class
RuleBasedMatcher
(
BaseMatcher
):
"""Rule-based matcher using weighted linear combination of attribute similarities.
This matcher implements the same functionality as Winter's LinearCombinationMatchingRule
but with PyDI's simpler DataFrame-first design. It computes a weighted sum of
similarity scores from multiple comparators to determine entity matches.
The comparators and weights are passed directly to the match() method following
the design principle of keeping the matcher stateless.
Example
-------
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching import RuleBasedMatcher
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching.comparators import jaccard, date_within_years
>>>
>>> matcher = RuleBasedMatcher()
>>> matches = matcher.match(
...     df_left, df_right, candidates,
...     comparators=[jaccard("title"), date_within_years("date", 2)],


Bases:
BaseMatcher
Machine learning-based entity matcher with scikit-learn integration.
This matcher uses trained scikit-learn classifiers to predict entity matches
based on feature vectors extracted from candidate pairs. It provides minimal
wrapping around scikit-learn while maintaining PyDI compatibility.
The matcher requires a FeatureExtractor to convert entity pairs into features
and a trained scikit-learn classifier. This design allows users to leverage
the full scikit-learn ecosystem (hyperparameter tuning, cross-validation,
feature selection, etc.) while maintaining PyDI’s DataFrame-first approach.

class
RuleBasedMatcher
(
BaseMatcher
):
"""Rule-based matcher using weighted linear combination of attribute similarities.
This matcher implements the same functionality as Winter's LinearCombinationMatchingRule
but with PyDI's simpler DataFrame-first design. It computes a weighted sum of
similarity scores from multiple comparators to determine entity matches.
The comparators and weights are passed directly to the match() method following
the design principle of keeping the matcher stateless.
Example
-------
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching import RuleBasedMatcher
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching.comparators import jaccard, date_within_years
>>>
>>> matcher = RuleBasedMatcher()
>>> matches = matcher.match(
...     df_left, df_right, candidates,
...     comparators=[jaccard("title"), date_within_years("date", 2)],

This matcher uses LangChain’s BaseChatModel interface to match schemas
by showing the LLM both datasets in markdown format and asking it to
identify column correspondences based on column names, data types, and
sample values.

Embedding-based blocking using nearest neighbor search over text embeddings.

computes embeddings using sentence transformers or a custom embedder,

class
RuleBasedMatcher
(
BaseMatcher
):
"""Rule-based matcher using weighted linear combination of attribute similarities.
This matcher implements the same functionality as Winter's LinearCombinationMatchingRule
but with PyDI's simpler DataFrame-first design. It computes a weighted sum of
similarity scores from multiple comparators to determine entity matches.
The comparators and weights are passed directly to the match() method following
the design principle of keeping the matcher stateless.
Example
-------
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching import RuleBasedMatcher
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching.comparators import jaccard, date_within_years
>>>
>>> matcher = RuleBasedMatcher()
>>> matches = matcher.match(
...     df_left, df_right, candidates,
...     comparators=[jaccard("title"), date_within_years("date", 2)],

"""LLM-based schema matcher using Large Language Models.
This matcher uses LangChain's BaseChatModel interface to match schemas
by showing the LLM both datasets in markdown format and asking it to
identify column correspondences based on column names, data types, and
sample values.

Rule-based entity matching using weighted linear combination of comparators.
"""

[docs]
class
RuleBasedMatcher
(
BaseMatcher
):
"""Rule-based matcher using weighted linear combination of attribute similarities.
This matcher implements the same functionality as Winter's LinearCombinationMatchingRule
but with PyDI's simpler DataFrame-first design. It computes a weighted sum of
similarity scores from multiple comparators to determine entity matches.
The comparators and weights are passed directly to the match() method following
the design principle of keeping the matcher stateless.
Example
-------
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching import RuleBasedMatcher
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching.comparators import jaccard, date_within_years
>>>
>>> matcher = RuleBasedMatcher()
>>> matches = matcher.match(
...     df_left, df_right, candidates,
...     comparators=[jaccard("title"), date_within_years("date", 2)],


Bases:
ABC
Abstract base class for streaming candidate generators.
Contract:
- Initialized with left/right DataFrames and strategy params
- Iterable yielding DataFrames with columns [“id1”, “id2”, “block_key?”].
- Each yielded batch should be <=
batch_size
rows.
-
estimate_pairs()
may return an estimate or None.
-
stats()
returns simple diagnostics.
-
materialize()
returns all candidates as a single DataFrame (for small data).

[source]

Bases:
BaseBlocker
Token-based blocking using token overlap on a text column.
Each record is assigned to blocks by its tokens. Candidate pairs are
generated when left/right records share at least one token. A simple
tokenizer is used by default, but a custom callable may be provided.
Also supports character and word ngrams for more flexible blocking strategies.
When using ngrams, records are blocked based on overlapping character sequences
or word sequences of the specified size.

class
SortedNeighbourhoodBlocker
(
BaseBlocker
):
"""Sorted neighbourhood blocking using a sliding window over a sort key.
Records from both datasets are combined into a single ordering based on the
provided key. Then, within a window of size `window`, cross-dataset pairs are
generated. The algorithm is linearithmic due to sorting.
"""

response:
and a trained scikit-learn classifier. This design allows users to leverage
the full scikit-learn ecosystem (hyperparameter tuning, cross-validation,
feature selection, etc.) while maintaining PyDI's DataFrame-first approach.
Parameters
----------
feature_extractor : FeatureExtractor or VectorFeatureExtractor
Feature extraction component that converts entity pairs to feature vectors.
Examples
--------
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching import MLBasedMatcher, FeatureExtractor
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching.comparators import StringComparator
>>> from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
>>>
>>> # Create feature extractor
>>> extractor = FeatureExtractor([
...     StringComparator("title", "jaro_winkler"),
...     NumericComparator("year", "absolute_difference")
... ])
>>>
>>> # Prepare training data
>>> train_features = extractor.create_features(df_left, df_right, train_pairs, train_labels)
>>> X = train_features.drop(['label', 'id1', 'id2'], axis=1)
>>> y = train_features['label']
>>>
>>> # Train classifier using full scikit-learn workflow
>>> clf = RandomForestClassifier(n_estimators=100)
>>> clf.fit(X, y)
>>>
>>> # Use trained classifier with matcher


Bases:
BaseMatcher
Machine learning-based entity matcher with scikit-learn integration.
This matcher uses trained scikit-learn classifiers to predict entity matches
based on feature vectors extracted from candidate pairs. It provides minimal
wrapping around scikit-learn while maintaining PyDI compatibility.
The matcher requires a FeatureExtractor to convert entity pairs into features
and a trained scikit-learn classifier. This design allows users to leverage
the full scikit-learn ecosystem (hyperparameter tuning, cross-validation,
feature selection, etc.) while maintaining PyDI’s DataFrame-first approach.

MaximumBipartiteMatching
(
BasePostClusterer
):
"""Finds optimal one-to-one matching using maximum bipartite matching.
This algorithm formulates the entity matching problem as a maximum weight
bipartite matching problem and finds the globally optimal solution using
the Hungarian algorithm or similar optimization methods.
Unlike the greedy approach, this guarantees the optimal solution that
maximizes the total similarity score while enforcing one-to-one constraints.

BaseBlocker
Embedding-based blocking using nearest neighbor search over text embeddings.
This blocker combines text columns into a single string representation,
computes embeddings using sentence transformers or a custom embedder,
and uses nearest neighbor search to find candidate pairs above a similarity threshold.

[source]

Bases:
BaseBlocker
Token-based blocking using token overlap on a text column.
Each record is assigned to blocks by its tokens. Candidate pairs are
generated when left/right records share at least one token. A simple
tokenizer is used by default, but a custom callable may be provided.
Also supports character and word ngrams for more flexible blocking strategies.
When using ngrams, records are blocked based on overlapping character sequences
or word sequences of the specified size.


Bases:
BaseBlocker
Sorted neighbourhood blocking using a sliding window over a sort key.
Records from both datasets are combined into a single ordering based on the

class
SortedNeighbourhoodBlocker
(
BaseBlocker
):
"""Sorted neighbourhood blocking using a sliding window over a sort key.
Records from both datasets are combined into a single ordering based on the
provided key. Then, within a window of size `window`, cross-dataset pairs are
generated. The algorithm is linearithmic due to sorting.
"""

Bases:
BaseMatcher
Machine learning-based entity matcher with scikit-learn integration.
This matcher uses trained scikit-learn classifiers to predict entity matches
based on feature vectors extracted from candidate pairs. It provides minimal
wrapping around scikit-learn while maintaining PyDI compatibility.
The matcher requires a FeatureExtractor to convert entity pairs into features
and a trained scikit-learn classifier. This design allows users to leverage
the full scikit-learn ecosystem (hyperparameter tuning, cross-validation,
feature selection, etc.) while maintaining PyDI’s DataFrame-first approach.

Returns

Dict[str, Any]
Dictionary containing blocking evaluation metrics:
- pair_completeness: float, fraction of true matches found in candidates (blocking recall)
- pair_quality: float, fraction of candidates that are true matches (blocking precision)
- reduction_ratio: float, reduction from total possible pairs (1 - candidates/total)
- total_candidates: int, number of candidate pairs generated
- total_possible_pairs: int, total possible pairs in search space
- true_positives_found: int, number of true matches in candidate set
- total_true_pairs: int, total number of true matches in test set
- evaluation_timestamp: str, ISO timestamp of evaluation

------
ValueError

provided key. Then, within a window of size

class
RuleBasedMatcher
(
BaseMatcher
):
"""Rule-based matcher using weighted linear combination of attribute similarities.
This matcher implements the same functionality as Winter's LinearCombinationMatchingRule
but with PyDI's simpler DataFrame-first design. It computes a weighted sum of
similarity scores from multiple comparators to determine entity matches.
The comparators and weights are passed directly to the match() method following
the design principle of keeping the matcher stateless.
Example
-------
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching import RuleBasedMatcher
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching.comparators import jaccard, date_within_years
>>>
>>> matcher = RuleBasedMatcher()
>>> matches = matcher.match(
...     df_left, df_right, candidates,
...     comparators=[jaccard("title"), date_within_years("date", 2)],

class
RuleBasedMatcher
(
BaseMatcher
):
"""Rule-based matcher using weighted linear combination of attribute similarities.
This matcher implements the same functionality as Winter's LinearCombinationMatchingRule
but with PyDI's simpler DataFrame-first design. It computes a weighted sum of
similarity scores from multiple comparators to determine entity matches.
The comparators and weights are passed directly to the match() method following
the design principle of keeping the matcher stateless.
Example
-------
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching import RuleBasedMatcher
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching.comparators import jaccard, date_within_years
>>>
>>> matcher = RuleBasedMatcher()
>>> matches = matcher.match(
...     df_left, df_right, candidates,
...     comparators=[jaccard("title"), date_within_years("date", 2)],


Register how to fuse an attribute (unified API).
Accepts either a pre-built AttributeValueFuser (advanced usage) or a
resolver function (simple usage). When a resolver function is provided,
it is wrapped into an AttributeValueFuser with any extra keyword args
supplied via
fuser_kwargs
(e.g., accessor=…, required=True).

BaseSchemaMatcher
LLM-based schema matcher using Large Language Models.
This matcher uses LangChain’s BaseChatModel interface to match schemas
by showing the LLM both datasets in markdown format and asking it to
identify column correspondences based on column names, data types, and
sample values.

EmbeddingBlocker
(
BaseBlocker
):
"""
Embedding-based blocking using nearest neighbor search over text embeddings.


Bases:
BaseBlocker
Token-based blocking using token overlap on a text column.
Each record is assigned to blocks by its tokens. Candidate pairs are
generated when left/right records share at least one token. A simple
tokenizer is used by default, but a custom callable may be provided.
Also supports character and word ngrams for more flexible blocking strategies.
When using ngrams, records are blocked based on overlapping character sequences
or word sequences of the specified size.

"""String similarity comparator using textdistance metrics.
Parameters
----------
column : str

similarity_function : str

Tokenization strategy for token-based similarity functions. Options:
- "word": Whitespace tokenization
- "char": Character-level tokenization
- "ngram_2", "ngram_3": N-gram tokenization
- callable: Custom tokenizer function
Default: "word" for token-based functions (jaccard, sorensen_dice, cosine, etc.),
"char" for edit-based functions (jaro_winkler, levenshtein, etc.).
preprocess : callable, optional

class
PyDI.entitymatching.
RuleBasedMatcher
[source]

Bases:
BaseMatcher
Rule-based matcher using weighted linear combination of attribute similarities.
This matcher implements the same functionality as Winter’s LinearCombinationMatchingRule
but with PyDI’s simpler DataFrame-first design. It computes a weighted sum of
similarity scores from multiple comparators to determine entity matches.
The comparators and weights are passed directly to the match() method following
the design principle of keeping the matcher stateless.
Example
>>>
from
PyDI.entitymatching
import
RuleBasedMatcher
>>>
from
PyDI.entitymatching.comparators
import
jaccard
,
date_within_years
>>>
>>>
matcher
=
RuleBasedMatcher
()
>>>
matches
=
matcher
.
match
(
...
df_left
,
df_right
,
candidates
,
...
comparators
=
[
jaccard
(
"title"
),
date_within_years
(
"date"
,
2
)],
...
weights
=
[
2
,
1
],
# relative importance (normalized to [0.67, 0.33])
...
threshold
=
0.7
...
)
match
(
df_left
,
df_right
,
candidates
,
id_column
,
comparators
,
weights
=
None
,
threshold
=
0.0
,
debug
=
False
,
**

-----

>>>
>>> matcher = RuleBasedMatcher()
>>> matches = matcher.match(
...     df_left, df_right, candidates,
...     comparators=[jaccard("title"), date_within_years("date", 2)],
...     weights=[2, 1],  # relative importance (normalized to [0.67, 0.33])
...     threshold=0.7
... )

class
RuleBasedMatcher
(
BaseMatcher
):
"""Rule-based matcher using weighted linear combination of attribute similarities.
This matcher implements the same functionality as Winter's LinearCombinationMatchingRule
but with PyDI's simpler DataFrame-first design. It computes a weighted sum of
similarity scores from multiple comparators to determine entity matches.
The comparators and weights are passed directly to the match() method following
the design principle of keeping the matcher stateless.
Example
-------
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching import RuleBasedMatcher
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching.comparators import jaccard, date_within_years
>>>
>>> matcher = RuleBasedMatcher()
>>> matches = matcher.match(
...     df_left, df_right, candidates,
...     comparators=[jaccard("title"), date_within_years("date", 2)],

-----

,

**kwargs

DataFrame with columns id1, id2, score, notes containing
entity correspondences above the threshold.

computes embeddings using sentence transformers or a custom embedder,

class
SortedNeighbourhoodBlocker
(
BaseBlocker
):
"""Sorted neighbourhood blocking using a sliding window over a sort key.
Records from both datasets are combined into a single ordering based on the
provided key. Then, within a window of size `window`, cross-dataset pairs are
generated. The algorithm is linearithmic due to sorting.
"""

Notes
The “relative_difference” method with max_difference is equivalent to Winter’s
PercentageSimilarity. For values differing by X%, where X < max_difference*100:
similarity = 1 - (X/100) / max_difference

BasePostClusterer
Finds optimal one-to-one matching using maximum bipartite matching.
This algorithm formulates the entity matching problem as a maximum weight
bipartite matching problem and finds the globally optimal solution using
the Hungarian algorithm or similar optimization methods.
Unlike the greedy approach, this guarantees the optimal solution that
maximizes the total similarity score while enforcing one-to-one constraints.
Requires NetworkX for the bipartite matching implementation.

Provenance-aware pandas I/O wrappers.

Add a unique identifier column to each loaded DataFrame

df.attrs["provenance"]
Optionally include minimal provenance columns (

,
__dataset_name
)
The functions follow the design guidelines in
CLAUDE.md
: pandas-first,
rich NumPy-style docstrings, and lightweight logging for observability.

class
RuleBasedMatcher
(
BaseMatcher
):
"""Rule-based matcher using weighted linear combination of attribute similarities.
This matcher implements the same functionality as Winter's LinearCombinationMatchingRule
but with PyDI's simpler DataFrame-first design. It computes a weighted sum of
similarity scores from multiple comparators to determine entity matches.
The comparators and weights are passed directly to the match() method following
the design principle of keeping the matcher stateless.
Example
-------
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching import RuleBasedMatcher
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching.comparators import jaccard, date_within_years
>>>
>>> matcher = RuleBasedMatcher()
>>> matches = matcher.match(
...     df_left, df_right, candidates,
...     comparators=[jaccard("title"), date_within_years("date", 2)],

"""String similarity comparator using textdistance metrics.
Parameters
----------
column : str
Column name to compare.
similarity_function : str
Name of textdistance similarity function.
tokenization : str or callable, optional
Tokenization strategy for token-based similarity functions. Options:
- "word": Whitespace tokenization
- "char": Character-level tokenization
- "ngram_2", "ngram_3": N-gram tokenization
- callable: Custom tokenizer function
Default: "word" for token-based functions (jaccard, sorensen_dice, cosine, etc.),
"char" for edit-based functions (jaro_winkler, levenshtein, etc.).
preprocess : callable, optional
Function to preprocess values before comparison.
list_strategy : str, optional
Strategy for handling lists ("concatenate", "best_match", "set_jaccard", "set_overlap").

class

(
BaseBlocker
):
"""Sorted neighbourhood blocking using a sliding window over a sort key.
Records from both datasets are combined into a single ordering based on the
provided key. Then, within a window of size `window`, cross-dataset pairs are
generated. The algorithm is linearithmic due to sorting.

class
RuleBasedMatcher
(
BaseMatcher
):
"""Rule-based matcher using weighted linear combination of attribute similarities.
This matcher implements the same functionality as Winter's LinearCombinationMatchingRule
but with PyDI's simpler DataFrame-first design. It computes a weighted sum of
similarity scores from multiple comparators to determine entity matches.
The comparators and weights are passed directly to the match() method following
the design principle of keeping the matcher stateless.
Example
-------
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching import RuleBasedMatcher
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching.comparators import jaccard, date_within_years
>>>
>>> matcher = RuleBasedMatcher()
>>> matches = matcher.match(
...     df_left, df_right, candidates,
...     comparators=[jaccard("title"), date_within_years("date", 2)],

-----

optional
) – Device for inference.

:
DataFrame with id1, id2, prediction columns.

:
pandas.DataFrame

[source]

Register how to fuse an attribute (unified API).
Accepts either a pre-built AttributeValueFuser (advanced usage) or a
resolver function (simple usage). When a resolver function is provided,
it is wrapped into an AttributeValueFuser with any extra keyword args
supplied via

- Initialized with left/right DataFrames and strategy params
- Iterable yielding DataFrames with columns [“id1”, “id2”, “block_key?”].

batch_size
rows.

-

class
PyDI.entitymatching.
RuleBasedMatcher
[source]

Bases:
BaseMatcher
Rule-based matcher using weighted linear combination of attribute similarities.
This matcher implements the same functionality as Winter’s LinearCombinationMatchingRule
but with PyDI’s simpler DataFrame-first design. It computes a weighted sum of
similarity scores from multiple comparators to determine entity matches.
The comparators and weights are passed directly to the match() method following
the design principle of keeping the matcher stateless.
Example
>>>
from
PyDI.entitymatching
import
RuleBasedMatcher
>>>
from
PyDI.entitymatching.comparators
import
jaccard
,
date_within_years
>>>
>>>
matcher
=
RuleBasedMatcher
()
>>>
matches
=
matcher
.
match
(
...
df_left
,
df_right
,
candidates
,
...
comparators
=
[
jaccard
(
"title"
),
date_within_years
(
"date"
,
2
)],
...
weights
=
[
2
,
1
],
# relative importance (normalized to [0.67, 0.33])
...
threshold
=
0.7
...

class
RuleBasedMatcher
(
BaseMatcher
):
"""Rule-based matcher using weighted linear combination of attribute similarities.
This matcher implements the same functionality as Winter's LinearCombinationMatchingRule
but with PyDI's simpler DataFrame-first design. It computes a weighted sum of
similarity scores from multiple comparators to determine entity matches.
The comparators and weights are passed directly to the match() method following
the design principle of keeping the matcher stateless.
Example
-------
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching import RuleBasedMatcher
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching.comparators import jaccard, date_within_years
>>>
>>> matcher = RuleBasedMatcher()
>>> matches = matcher.match(
...     df_left, df_right, candidates,
...     comparators=[jaccard("title"), date_within_years("date", 2)],

class
PyDI.entitymatching.
RuleBasedMatcher
[source]

Bases:
BaseMatcher
Rule-based matcher using weighted linear combination of attribute similarities.
This matcher implements the same functionality as Winter’s LinearCombinationMatchingRule
but with PyDI’s simpler DataFrame-first design. It computes a weighted sum of
similarity scores from multiple comparators to determine entity matches.
The comparators and weights are passed directly to the match() method following
the design principle of keeping the matcher stateless.
Example
>>>
from
PyDI.entitymatching
import
RuleBasedMatcher
>>>
from
PyDI.entitymatching.comparators
import
jaccard
,
date_within_years
>>>
>>>
matcher
=
RuleBasedMatcher
()
>>>
matches
=
matcher
.
match
(
...
df_left
,
df_right
,
candidates
,
...
comparators
=
[
jaccard
(
"title"
),
date_within_years
(
"date"
,
2
)],
...
weights
=
[
2
,
1
],
# relative importance (normalized to [0.67, 0.33])
...
threshold
=
0.7
...
)

(
df_left
,
df_right
,
candidates
,
id_column
,
comparators
,

=
None
,

=
0.0
,

=

,

class
RuleBasedMatcher
(
BaseMatcher
):
"""Rule-based matcher using weighted linear combination of attribute similarities.
This matcher implements the same functionality as Winter's LinearCombinationMatchingRule
but with PyDI's simpler DataFrame-first design. It computes a weighted sum of
similarity scores from multiple comparators to determine entity matches.
The comparators and weights are passed directly to the match() method following
the design principle of keeping the matcher stateless.
Example
-------
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching import RuleBasedMatcher
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching.comparators import jaccard, date_within_years
>>>
>>> matcher = RuleBasedMatcher()
>>> matches = matcher.match(
...     df_left, df_right, candidates,
...     comparators=[jaccard("title"), date_within_years("date", 2)],

-----

,
optional
) – Device for inference.
Returns
:
DataFrame with id1, id2, prediction columns.
Return type
:
pandas.DataFrame

class
RuleBasedMatcher
(
BaseMatcher
):
"""Rule-based matcher using weighted linear combination of attribute similarities.
This matcher implements the same functionality as Winter's LinearCombinationMatchingRule
but with PyDI's simpler DataFrame-first design. It computes a weighted sum of
similarity scores from multiple comparators to determine entity matches.
The comparators and weights are passed directly to the match() method following
the design principle of keeping the matcher stateless.
Example
-------
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching import RuleBasedMatcher
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching.comparators import jaccard, date_within_years
>>>
>>> matcher = RuleBasedMatcher()
>>> matches = matcher.match(
...     df_left, df_right, candidates,
...     comparators=[jaccard("title"), date_within_years("date", 2)],

Rule-based entity matching using weighted linear combination of comparators.
"""

[docs]
class
RuleBasedMatcher
(
BaseMatcher
):
"""Rule-based matcher using weighted linear combination of attribute similarities.
This matcher implements the same functionality as Winter's LinearCombinationMatchingRule
but with PyDI's simpler DataFrame-first design. It computes a weighted sum of
similarity scores from multiple comparators to determine entity matches.
The comparators and weights are passed directly to the match() method following
the design principle of keeping the matcher stateless.
Example
-------
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching import RuleBasedMatcher
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching.comparators import jaccard, date_within_years
>>>
>>> matcher = RuleBasedMatcher()
>>> matches = matcher.match(
...     df_left, df_right, candidates,
...     comparators=[jaccard("title"), date_within_years("date", 2)],

class
RuleBasedMatcher
(
BaseMatcher
):
"""Rule-based matcher using weighted linear combination of attribute similarities.
This matcher implements the same functionality as Winter's LinearCombinationMatchingRule
but with PyDI's simpler DataFrame-first design. It computes a weighted sum of
similarity scores from multiple comparators to determine entity matches.
The comparators and weights are passed directly to the match() method following
the design principle of keeping the matcher stateless.
Example
-------
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching import RuleBasedMatcher
>>> from PyDI.entitymatching.comparators import jaccard, date_within_years
>>>
>>> matcher = RuleBasedMatcher()
>>> matches = matcher.match(
...     df_left, df_right, candidates,
...     comparators=[jaccard("title"), date_within_years("date", 2)],

ABC
Abstract base class for streaming candidate generators.
Contract:
- Initialized with left/right DataFrames and strategy params
- Iterable yielding DataFrames with columns [“id1”, “id2”, “block_key?”].
- Each yielded batch should be <=
batch_size
rows.
-
estimate_pairs()
may return an estimate or None.
-
stats()
returns simple diagnostics.
-
materialize()
returns all candidates as a single DataFrame (for small data).

Bases:
BaseBlocker
Embedding-based blocking using nearest neighbor search over text embeddings.
This blocker combines text columns into a single string representation,
computes embeddings using sentence transformers or a custom embedder,
and uses nearest neighbor search to find candidate pairs above a similarity threshold.

This module provides comprehensive data fusion capabilities including:
